profile.name: Abid Shahriar
profile.title: Jr. Software Engineer
profile.about: Hi! I am Abid Shahriar. I have 2 years of experience in writting backend API. I am familiar with Ruby, Python, C++, C. During my academia I've built multiple projects using PHP. I am adaptable and naturally inclined toward learning new technologies. In my free time I solve coding problems on online judges. I recommend to check my Leetcode, Codeforces, Hackerrank profile. I am passionate about exploring the world of AGI & LLM. I also used to make 3D stuff once upon a time. Also check my Artstation profile.

skills.experience: I have written several Python projects from The Big Book of Small Python Projects, that gave me a good grasp of OOP with python. In my previous company, I worked with Grape, a Ruby-based API framework, to build RESTful APIs. During my academic years, I primarily used raw PHP to build various web applications. I use C++ during competitive programming and problem-solving, apart from that I have basic understanding of C.

I built a number of projects using Ruby on Rails during my internship. Aditionally, I have experience working with Grape, a REST-like API framework. In my industry career I mostly worked with PostgreSQL, but I also worked with MySQL on bunch of other projects. Currently, I am working on a personal project using FastAPI.

During my academic years, I used Bootstrap and CSS to build basic user interfaces for my projects. I designed my portfolio using Astro and Tailwind CSS, and working extensively with Tailwind's utility classes helped me gain a better understanding of several CSS concepts that I had previously found confusing. I also worked with React.js during my research assistantship at L A Technologies.

As a software engineer, I use Git, Postman for API testing, and Linux-based environments in my daily workflow. Outside of software development, I have spent time creating 3D stuff. Some of which are available on my ArtStation profile. During that time, I worked with Blender and Autodesk Maya to design hard surface.

works: Designed resource-oriented RESTful APIs following HTTP standards, improving frontend integration and data consistency.
Broke down complex business features into maintainable units, improving development efficiency.
Implemented bulk data import and CSV export functionality in multiple systems.
Built a modular API design pattern with Grape and Entities for uniform response structures.
Optimized slow queries and fixed N+1 issues in production APIs.
Deepened Rails expertise through hands-on development and maintenance.
Built service layer and integrated third-party APIs for external data processing.
Built Depot, a shopping cart app from Agile Web Development with Rails.
Implemented database associations and form handling for cart and checkout flows.
Managed user sessions and applied Rails best practices throughout the project.
Built another e-commerce backend API using Grape.
Followed company standards for API structure and response handling.
Worked in a project funded by Khulna University Research Cell under supervision.
Contributed to an open-source vehicle tracking system using React.js and Java.
Developed vehicle management, route creation, and scheduling features.
Managed a team of 3, handling project planning, task delegation, and delivery.
Created scripts to automate common tasks and improve development productivity.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science & Engineering
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science & Engineering
education.research: Development of a System for Detection and Extraction of Camera Inserted Date from Images.
education.courses: C, Data Structures, Algorithms, Digital Logic Design, Computer Fundamentals, Numerical Methods, Computer Graphics, Pattern Recognition, Machine Learning, Other Core CSE Courses

education.institute: Govt. Mojid Memorial City College, Khulna

projects.title: Labbaik
projects.description: This is a comprehensive pilgrim companion app by Ministry of Religious Affairs of Bangladesh to facilitate the hajj journey for BD pilgrims. I worked as a backend developer in this project before the release of first version. Key features of this app are providig Quran, Hadith library, location tracking, pilgrim health profile and many others. This app has been crossed 50k+ downloads on Google Play.

projects.title: Amar Shodai
projects.description: Amar shodai is a local e-commerce platform that enables customers to order groceries and everyday essentials online. It offers a wide range of products, including fresh vegetables, fruits, medicines, and household items, with reliable doorstep delivery.

projects.title: Smart Vehicle tracking & monitoring system for Khulna University
projects.description: Smart vehicle tracking is customized as organizational vehicle tracking and monitoring platform that provides real-time GPS tracking, route replay, trip history, and customizable fuel reporting. Our solution got 2nd palce in Synergy X Innovation Showcase 2024 held in Khulna University.

projects.title: Conference Management System
projects.description: Conference Management System is a web application that streamlines the academic conference workflow. It supports user authentication, paper submission, peer review, notifications, and role-based access for students, authors, and reviewers. This project was developed as part of the Web Programming Laboratory course.

projects.title: Restaurant management system
projects.description: It was more of a event management web application. People can create event and reserve restaurant through this application managed by an admin. This project was developerd as part of Database Management System Laboratory course.

projects.title: Pymer (Desktop timer)
projects.description: A countdown timer with Start, pause, and reset controls time's up alarm, always-on-top mode clean and minimal user interface lightweight that has been developed out of my need. It is available on Snap store.

certifications.description: These certifications were earned through online courses with hands-on experience and practical assignments.
